conditions: Closest 4ft piling at right edge is sharp with clear texture. The ~11ft piling behind it is visible but quite hazy. The 14ft and 30ft pilings are essentially lost in the background haze with no clear silhouette. This aligns with conditions where only the 11ft piling is meaningfully visible beyond the nearest, indicating about 10 ft visibility.

Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is clear with good texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but soft, and the 14 ft piling on the left appears as a broad, hazy silhouette with little detail. No clear view of 30 ft pilings or bottom. This matches conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded.

Closest 4ft and 11ft pilings on the right are clear with good texture. The 14ft piling on the left is visible but hazy, and the far 30ft pilings in the center are only very faint silhouettes. The sandy bottom is clearly visible near the camera. This matches the guideline where 30ft pilings are faint silhouettes, indicating ~25 ft visibility.

Closest 4 ft piling on right is sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft/14 ft pilings further back are visible but appear as hazy silhouettes with little detail, similar to the 15–20 ft guideline rather than the 25 ft reference where 30 ft pilings are discernible. No clear 30 ft pilings visible.

Closest 4ft piling at right is very sharp with clear texture. The 11ft/14ft pilings farther back are visible but somewhat hazy, with less distinct detail and more silhouette-like than in the 20–35 ft examples. The sandy bottom is visible immediately below but there is no clear view of the far (~30ft) pilings. This matches conditions where only the nearer mid pilings are reliably visible, suggesting around 15 ft visibility.
